title: Mandatory Publishing, Returns, Alternate Exchanges, and Route Audits · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first unroutable publication detection and ownership record for mandatory publishing, returns, alternate exchanges, and route audits. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of unroutable publication detection and ownership record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Register return handling before mandatory publication and correlate returned content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.
Distinguish broker confirms from consumer processing and combine mandatory returns or alternate routing with confirm results.
Build topology fixtures that assert expected destination sets, forbidden routes, unroutable cases, permissions, and changed bindings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Mandatory Return Sequence
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 6816 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep mandatory return, alternate custody, confirmation, and route uncertainty separate; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Register return handling before mandatory publication and correlate returned content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Alternate Return Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Alternate Return Tradeoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop messaging team handles case 1245 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep mandatory return, alternate custody, confirmation, and route uncertainty separate; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.
